subject: UX & Research
title: Flat vs. Deep Hierarchies
description: A short lesson to help you apply Flat vs. Deep Hierarchies.

hook.text: A site with 3 levels and 8 items each has 512 pages. With 6 levels and 3 items: 729 pages. Which is easier?

buildup.text: Flat hierarchies show many options at each level. Deep hierarchies show few options but require more clicks.

discovery.text: Research shows users prefer wider, shallower structures. Scanning 8 clear options beats clicking through 6 levels.

twist.text: But if labels are ambiguous, flat structures force harder choices. Users can't tell which of 8 vague options to pick.

climax.text: The answer depends on label clarity. Clear labels favor flat structures. Ambiguous labels need deeper paths.

punchline.text: Flat if labels are clear. Deep if they're ambiguous. Clarity decides structure.

quiz.question: When should you choose a deeper hierarchy over a flatter one?
options: When top-level labels are ambiguous and users need narrowing steps to find the right path
When the site has fewer than 100 pages total
Deep hierarchies are always better for SEO
